name: Raisin English Muffins
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve yeast in water in a large mixing bowl; let stand 5 minutes.
2. Combine milk, sugar, salt, and butter in a small saucepan. Cook over medium-low heat, stirring until butter melts. Cool to warm (105 to 115°F).
3. Stir the milk mixture, raisins, and 3 cups flour into the yeast mixture; beat until smooth. Add 2-1/2 to 3 cups of flour to form a stiff dough.
4. Turn the dough out onto a floured surface, and knead 2 minutes or until the dough can be shaped into a ball (dough will be slightly sticky). Place in a well-greased bowl, turning to grease the top. Cover and let rise in a warm place (85°F), free of drafts, about 1 hour or until doubled in bulk.
5. Punch down the dough, and divide it in half. Turn each half out onto a smooth surface heavily sprinkled with cornmeal. Pat one half of dough into a circle, ½ inch thick, using palms of hands; cut dough into rounds with a 2-3/4-inch cutter (cut carefully, as any leftover dough should not be reused).
6. Sprinkle 2 baking sheets with cornmeal. Transfer the cut dough rounds to baking sheets, placing 2 inches apart with cornmeal side down (one side of dough should remain free of cornmeal). Repeat process with remaining half of dough.
7. Cover and let rise in a warm place (85°F), free of drafts, for 30 minutes or until doubled in bulk.
8. Using a wide spatula, transfer rounds to a preheated 360°F electric skillet. Place cornmeal side down; cook for 6 minutes. Turn and cook an additional 6 minutes. Cool on wire racks.
9. To serve, split muffins and toast until lightly browned. Store muffins in an airtight container.
